user.title: Environment Canada Weather
user.description: Search for your city to set up weather data. The nearest city to your Home Assistant location is pre-filled.

select_city.title: Select City
select_city.description: Multiple cities matched your search. Select the correct one.

confirm.title: Confirm Settings
confirm.description: Confirm the settings for {city_name} ({province}) — city code: {city_code}. Auto-discovered values are pre-filled — edit if needed.

precip.title: Yesterday's Precipitation
precip.description: Optionally show how much rain or snow fell yesterday, measured at the nearest reporting Environment Canada station. Stations that report rain and snow separately give more detail; combined stations report a single total (with snow counted as its melted water equivalent in mm). The nearest station may be some distance away — pick whichever you prefer, or skip this feature.

error.no_city_found: No city found — try a different spelling (accents matter for French names).
error.api_error: Could not reach the Environment Canada API. Please try again.

abort.already_configured: Environment Canada Weather is already configured.

data_description.extended_forecast: Shows 7 extra days beyond Environment Canada's official 7-day forecast. The extra days are a longer-range outlook and are less precise: temperatures and rain chances are estimates, not exact values, and there is less daily detail. They appear dimmed in the card to set them apart.

beta.name: Beta
beta.description: Features being tested before they become official. Off by default; they may change substantially or be removed in a later release, and they may not work perfectly yet. Turning them off restores the stable behaviour. Feedback through GitHub issues is appreciated.

data_description.model_precip_estimate: Shows a model-derived expected daily precipitation total when Environment Canada does not state an amount. It is computed by weighting Environment Canada's hourly model amounts by their hourly chance of precipitation. Very small totals are hidden as noise.
data_description.ai_grouping: When several alerts describe the same weather event, ask an AI Task to group them so the card shows one alert with its related bulletins nested inside. Nothing is ever hidden or dropped. If the AI is unavailable, alerts simply appear ungrouped.
data_description.ai_task_entity: Which AI Task entity to use. Leave empty to use your preferred AI Task entity.
data_description.ai_grouping_instructions: How the AI decides which alerts belong together. The default works well; edit it only if you want to change the grouping judgment.

precip.title: Yesterday's Precipitation
precip.description: Choose the station used for yesterday's rain and snow, or opt out. Split stations report rain and snow separately; combined stations report a single total (snow as its melted water equivalent in mm). The nearest reporting station may be some distance away.

choose.title: Yesterday's Precipitation
choose.description: Environment Canada can report how much rain or snow fell yesterday at the nearest reporting station. Split stations report rain and snow separately; combined stations report a single total (snow as its melted water equivalent in mm). The nearest station may be some distance away — pick one to enable the feature, or opt out.
